title: The Queen's Loss (Part I)
titleZh: 女王失去的一切（第一部）

q.en: At the beginning of Part I, whom is the narrator trying to free from the sand illusion?
q.zh: 在第一部开头，叙述者试图把谁从沙之幻象中解救出来？

options.en: Anna, who believes she is holding a baby in a bundle of blankets
options.zh: 安娜，她相信自己怀里毯子中包着一个婴儿

options.en: The Queen, who is trapped on her throne
options.zh: 被困在王座上的女王

options.en: Jake, who is riding a steed
options.zh: 骑在骏马上的 Jake

options.en: Sarah, who is laughing on a couch
options.zh: 在沙发上欢笑的 Sarah

explain.en: The group crowds around Anna, who 'continues to coo at the baby she believes is wriggling in the empty bundle of blankets,' and the narrator works to break the illusion.
explain.zh: 众人围住安娜，她“仍对着她以为在毯子里扭动的孩子发出咕咕声”，叙述者努力打破幻象。

q.en: What does the Queen offer Qynn and her friends?
q.zh: 女王向 Qynn 和她的朋友们提出了什么？

options.en: A life of endless happiness in their own personal paradise, with no pain, sadness, or loss
options.zh: 在自己专属的乐园里永享幸福，没有痛苦、悲伤或失去

options.en: A free trip back home immediately
options.zh: 立刻免费送他们回家

options.en: A job working for the Queen
options.zh: 一份为女王工作的差事

options.en: A battle to the death
options.zh: 一场生死决斗

explain.en: The Queen says she can give them 'endless happiness' in 'your own personal paradise' with 'No pain, sadness ... or loss.'
explain.zh: 女王说她能让他们在“自己专属的乐园”里获得“无尽的幸福”，“没有痛苦、悲伤……或失去”。

q.en: What does Seth argue about happiness and sadness?
q.zh: 关于幸福与悲伤，Seth 提出了什么观点？

options.en: They are opposite sides of the same coin—one cannot exist without the other
options.zh: 它们是一枚硬币的两面——缺一不可

options.en: Happiness is the only real feeling
options.zh: 幸福才是唯一真实的感受

options.en: Sadness should always be avoided at any cost
options.zh: 应当不惜一切代价避开悲伤

options.en: They have nothing to do with each other
options.zh: 两者毫无关系

explain.en: Seth says 'Happiness and sadness are opposite sides of the same coin—one doesn't exist without the other.'
explain.zh: Seth 说“幸福与悲伤是一枚硬币的两面——缺一则另一者也不存在”。

q.en: What happens at the end of Part I after Qynn refuses the Queen's offer?
q.zh: 在第一部结尾，Qynn 拒绝女王的提议后发生了什么？

options.en: The Queen says she has no further need of them and commands, 'Be gone'
options.zh: 女王说不再需要他们，并命令“滚出去”

options.en: The Queen throws a victory party
options.zh: 女王举办了一场胜利派对

options.en: The Queen frees Jake and Sarah immediately
options.zh: 女王立刻释放了 Jake 和 Sarah

options.en: The Queen apologizes and leaves with them
options.zh: 女王道歉并和他们一起离开

explain.en: After Qynn rejects the offer, the Queen says 'Since you have rejected my offer, I have no further need of you,' and then 'Be gone.'
explain.zh: Qynn 拒绝后，“女王说……‘既然你拒绝了我的提议，我不再需要你们了，’……‘滚出去。’”

q.en: This story is told from a first-person point of view (the narrator says 'I'). How does this affect the reading experience?
q.zh: 这个故事以第一人称（叙述者用“我”）讲述。这对阅读体验有什么影响？

options.en: Readers experience events and feelings through Qynn's own eyes and thoughts
options.zh: 读者通过 Qynn 自己的所见所感和思想来经历事件

options.en: Readers learn the private thoughts of every character equally
options.zh: 读者平等地得知每个角色的内心想法

options.en: The story is told by an outside, all-knowing narrator
options.zh: 故事由一个置身事外的全知叙述者讲述

options.en: There is no narrator at all
options.zh: 根本没有叙述者

explain.en: The book uses 'I' throughout (e.g., 'I kneel down before Anna,' 'I reply'), letting readers share the narrator's perspective and inner thoughts.
explain.zh: 全书都用“我”（如“我在安娜面前跪下”“我答道”），让读者共享叙述者的视角与内心想法。

q.en: The narrator repeats the phrase 'It's not real' many times. What is the author's purpose for this repeated line?
q.zh: 叙述者反复说“这不是真的”（It's not real）。作者反复使用这句话的目的是什么？

options.en: To show Qynn using the phrase as a way to stay grounded in reality and resist the illusion
options.zh: 表现 Qynn 用这句话让自己锚定现实、抵抗幻象

options.en: To prove the baby was actually real
options.zh: 为了证明那个婴儿其实是真实的

options.en: To describe the weather
options.zh: 为了描述天气

options.en: To show she has forgotten her name
options.zh: 为了说明她忘了自己的名字

explain.en: Qynn reminds herself 'It's not real' and says it aloud to break Anna free, using the phrase to hold onto what is true amid the illusion.
explain.zh: Qynn 提醒自己“这不是真的”，并大声说出来以解救安娜，用这句话在幻象中抓住真实。

q.en: When the narrator enters the illusion of the nursery, she feels it is 'more authentic' than her real life. What does this reveal?
q.zh: 当叙述者进入育儿室的幻象时，她觉得它比真实生活“更真实”。这揭示了什么？

options.en: The sand illusions are so convincing and pleasant that they can feel more real than reality itself
options.zh: 沙之幻象极其逼真且美好，以至于让人觉得比现实还真实

options.en: The narrator prefers the dirty real world
options.zh: 叙述者更喜欢肮脏的现实世界

options.en: The nursery is actually a real place in another country
options.zh: 育儿室其实是另一个国家真实存在的地方

options.en: The Queen cannot create convincing illusions
options.zh: 女王无法制造逼真的幻象

explain.en: The narrator is 'nearly overwhelmed by the sensation that this place ... is more authentic than the existence that I had come from,' showing how powerful the illusions are.
explain.zh: 叙述者“几乎被一种感觉淹没：这地方……比我所来的现实更真实”，说明幻象的力量有多强。

q.en: The Queen shows Qynn two stained-glass windows—one with Sarah and her mom, one with Jake on a steed. What does this reveal about her method?
q.zh: 女王给 Qynn 看了两扇彩绘玻璃窗——一扇是 Sarah 和妈妈，一扇是 Jake 骑在骏马上。这揭示了她的什么手法？

options.en: She uses images of people's loved ones and deepest desires to tempt them into staying
options.zh: 她利用人们所爱之人与最深的渴望来诱惑他们留下

options.en: She shows them maps of the real world
options.zh: 她给他们看真实世界的地图

options.en: She is showing them the weather forecast
options.zh: 她在给他们看天气预报

options.en: She is punishing them with scary images
options.zh: 她用可怕的图像惩罚他们

explain.en: The Queen displays 'Sarah and her mom' laughing and 'Jake galloping on a great steed' to argue how blissful their personal paradises would be.
explain.zh: 女王展示“Sarah 和妈妈”欢笑、以及“Jake 骑着骏马奔驰”，以此论证他们的专属乐园会有多幸福。

q.en: Qynn argues that experiencing fear, pain, and sadness helps people 'learn and grow.' What is the author's purpose for this idea?
q.zh: Qynn 认为经历恐惧、痛苦和悲伤能让人“学习并成长”。作者安排这一想法的目的是什么？

options.en: To convey the theme that struggles and loss are part of being alive and help us value happiness
options.zh: 传达主题：挣扎与失去是活着的一部分，并帮助我们珍惜幸福

options.en: To say that people should seek out pain on purpose
options.zh: 为了说人们应当刻意去寻找痛苦

options.en: To prove happiness is impossible
options.zh: 为了证明幸福不可能存在

options.en: To suggest ignoring all negative feelings
options.zh: 为了建议忽略所有负面情绪

explain.en: The focus question asks what Qynn discovers 'about true happiness and illusions'; she concludes pain and loss push people 'to become strong' and grow.
explain.zh: 焦点问题是 Qynn 对“真正的幸福与幻象”有何发现；她得出结论：痛苦与失去推动人“变得坚强”并成长。

q.en: The title is 'The Queen's Loss.' What does Part I suggest the Queen is trying to avoid?
q.zh: 标题是“女王失去的一切”。第一部暗示女王在试图逃避什么？

options.en: She avoids pain and loss by creating illusions of endless happiness instead of facing real life
options.zh: 她通过制造无尽幸福的幻象来逃避痛苦与失去，而不敢面对真实生活

options.en: She is trying to avoid winning any battles
options.zh: 她在试图避开任何胜利

options.en: She wants to lose her throne on purpose
options.zh: 她想故意失去王位

options.en: She fears learning to read
options.zh: 她害怕学会阅读

explain.en: The Queen offers a world 'filled with nothing but joy. No pain, sadness ... or loss,' showing she uses illusions to escape the pain of loss.
explain.zh: 女王提供一个“只有欢乐、没有痛苦、悲伤……或失去”的世界，说明她用幻象来逃避失去之痛。

prompt.en: Qynn believes that sadness and happiness need each other, and that pain helps us grow. Do you agree? Describe a time you learned something important from a difficult or sad experience.
prompt.zh: Qynn 相信悲伤与幸福相辅相成，痛苦能助人成长。你同意吗？描述一次你从困难或悲伤的经历中学到重要东西的时刻。

en: Think about a challenge you faced and what it taught you.
Consider whether avoiding all sadness would really make life better.
zh: 想想你面对过的挑战，以及它教会了你什么。
思考如果避开所有悲伤，生活是否真的会更好。
